CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT, str VARCHAR);
SELECT id, str, strlen(str) AS length FROM test_strings;
SELECT id, str, strlen(str) AS length FROM test_strings WHERE strlen(str) > 3;
SELECT id, str, strlen(str) AS length FROM test_strings WHERE str LIKE '%D%';
SELECT id, str, strlen(str) AS length FROM test_strings WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_strings WHERE str LIKE '%uck%');
SELECT id, str, strlen(COALESCE(str, 'default')) AS length FROM test_strings;
SELECT id, str, CASE WHEN strlen(str) > 5 THEN 'Long' ELSE 'Short' END AS length_category FROM test_strings;
WITH lengths AS (SELECT id, strlen(str) AS length FROM test_strings) SELECT AVG(length) AS avg_length FROM lengths;
SELECT id, str, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings AS t2 WHERE strlen(t2.str) > strlen(t1.str)) AS longer_count FROM test_strings AS t1;
CREATE TABLE another_strings (id INT, str VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO another_strings VALUES (1, 'Longer string example'), (2, 'Short'), (3, '🐦');
SELECT a.id, a.str, strlen(a.str) AS length, b.id, b.str, strlen(b.str) AS other_length FROM test_strings AS a JOIN another_strings AS b ON a.id = b.id;
SELECT a.id, a.str, strlen(a.str) AS length FROM test_strings AS a WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM another_strings AS b WHERE strlen(a.str) < strlen(b.str));
SELECT id, str, strlen(str) AS length FROM test_strings WHERE strlen(str) BETWEEN 1 AND 10;
